heartbeat_policy.rule: A formal run may record either manual resume mode or a verified one-minute host heartbeat binding. Heartbeat wakeups append a tick; if the work chain is broken or unknown, the router re-enters resume recovery instead of continuing from chat memory.
heartbeat_policy.completion_action: When PM terminal closure succeeds, the router writes the closure suite and disables the active continuation binding. This repository upgrade task does not keep a live heartbeat automation after completion.

non_ui_upgrade_2026_05_05.scope: User asked to complete the first eight non-UI upgrade items. UI/Cockpit implementation is explicitly left to the user.
implemented_capabilities: Continuation binding now records manual resume or verified one-minute heartbeat mode.
Heartbeat wakeups distinguish alive work chains from broken or unknown chains and re-enter resume recovery when needed.
PM node acceptance requires a structured high-standard recheck before worker dispatch.
Final ledger construction rebuilds source-of-truth entries from the frozen root contract replay, effective route nodes, child-skill gates, ledgers, and mutation history.
Parent backward review failures mutate the route, mark superseded nodes, write stale-evidence records, and force review restart from the new repair node.
Effective route nodes auto-advance after node completion instead of relying on chat history.
Terminal backward replay is segmented and every segment requires reviewer review plus PM segment decision before closure.
If ledgers become dirty after terminal replay but before closure, the route completion chain is invalidated and restarts from PM evidence quality instead of delivering the closure card.
PM closure writes a terminal closure suite, blocks dirty lifecycle ledgers, and disables continuation binding only after clean closure.
known_non_ui_gaps: Generalized asynchronous officer packet dispatch is represented by cards and models, but not expanded into a full packet runtime family.
Old-state import quarantine is still a startup/resume policy surface rather than a full migration importer.
Final user-report generation remains an artifact expansion after closure; UI-specific evidence remains out of scope for this pass.

steps.title: Lock new architecture boundary
done_when: The launcher remains small and does not bulk-read references.
The three-question startup gate cannot proceed to banner or run shell before answers.
Controller remains relay-only and cannot approve, implement, or read sealed bodies.
System-card delivery still requires manifest checks.

steps.title: Minimal startup hard gate
evidence: Startup fact checks require run authority, display mode, crew freshness, continuation binding, and old-state quarantine boundaries.
Scheduled continuation must be represented by verified one-minute binding; otherwise the run records manual resume mode.

steps.title: Material intake, research, and PM understanding
Product function architecture and root contract
evidence: Root acceptance contract remains frozen before route activation.
Final ledger replay now proves every frozen root requirement from source-of-truth entries.

steps.title: Child skills, capability routing, and dependency policy
evidence: Child-skill selection and gate-manifest cards remain PM-owned and reviewer/officer checked.
Dependency policy is now a runtime PM card for current-node, reversible, justified dependency handling.
Officer request/report lifecycle has a focused PM card and updated model coverage; the generalized packet runtime remains a later expansion.

steps.title: Multi-node route tree and frontier
evidence: Route activation writes run-scoped route/frontier state.
Current-node packets are tied to active node and route version.
Parent nodes with children require parent backward replay and PM segment decision before completion.
Reviewer blocks rewrite the frontier to a repair node.
Effective route nodes now auto-advance after completion and reset per-node flags for the next node.

steps.title: Full packet/mail system for workers, reviewers, and officers
evidence: Material scan packets use physical packet envelopes and bodies.
Research packages use physical packet/result envelopes before reviewer direct-source checks.
Current-node worker packets and results are relayed by Controller through envelope-only ledger actions.
Officer modelability gates remain role-scoped runtime gates; a PM officer-request/report card and model coverage are present, while generalized async officer packets remain the next expansion.

steps.title: Evidence, quality package, and UI/visual evidence
evidence: PM evidence quality package writes evidence, generated-resource, and quality ledgers.
Stale/unresolved evidence and pending generated resources are rejected.
UI/visual routes require screenshot evidence and reject reused old assets as current proof.
Reviewer evidence-quality pass is required before final ledger work.

steps.title: Pause, resume, heartbeat, and lifecycle
evidence: Resume re-entry is a router-controlled packet-loop gate.
Controller loads persisted state but cannot infer progress from chat history or sealed bodies.
PM resume decision is required before continuation; ambiguous state blocks unsafe continuation.
One-minute heartbeat binding is recorded when authorized and supported; heartbeat ticks re-enter recovery when work-chain status is broken or unknown.
Terminal closure disables the continuation binding.

steps.title: Final ledger, terminal replay, and completion
evidence: PM final route-wide ledger writer validates clean evidence/resource/residual-risk counts.
Final ledger entries are rebuilt from source-of-truth artifacts rather than PM summaries.
Terminal human backward replay map is generated from the PM ledger and split into explicit review segments.
Every terminal segment requires reviewer review and PM continue decision.
PM closure writes a terminal closure suite and blocks dirty evidence/resource/final ledgers before closing the run.
